title: Champhaiah Nasha Mukt Bharat Saptah (Stuff Free Week) khar a ni
content:
1202/2026-2027

Dated Champhai, 25th June 2026:Vawiin khan Circuit House, Champhaiah Nasha Mukt Bharat Saptah (Drug Free Week, 2026) kharna neih a ni a, Pu Mohammad Aaquib, Chairman, NMBA leh Champhai District Bawhrsap ni bawk chu khuallian a ni.

Bawrhsap Pu Mohammad Aaquib chuan damdawi hmansualna tihtawp chu he programme hian a tum ber a nih thu a sawi a, Champhai hi ramri a nih angin damdawi a hluarin, tam tak man a nih thin thu a tarlang a, thlatin NCORD thutkhawm ah ruihhlo tam tak thlatin man a nih dan report ngaihthlak thin a nih thu a sawi a, hei hian Champhai mipuite chu ruihhlo do kawngah tha thawh vek a tul zia a lan tir thu a sawi a, Champhai mai bakah Mizoram puma Home hrang hrangah damdawi hmansual avanga enkawl mi tam tak an awm thu a sawi a.

Damdawi hmansualna hi hote a kan ngaih vaihlo leh a kaihhnawih atanga bul tan a nih tlangpui thu sawiin damdawi hman sual loh leh vaihlo lam hrim hrim khawih loh hi kan hriselna atana thil pawimawh tak a ni, a ti a, a bik takin thalaite chu ruihhlo hrang hrang laka fihlim turin a ngen a, an lo ti tawh a nih pawhin an theihna zawng zawng hmanga ruihhlo bawih atanga talchhuah tum turin a chah bawk. Champhai tana thalai duhawm leh tangkai an nih theihna tura ruihhlo laka fihlim ngei turin duhsakna a hlan a, Champhai Buffalo te'n an support thinnaah lawmthu a sawi bawk.

He hun hi Dr. Peter Malsawmtluanga, SDO (Sadar) chuan kaihruaiin Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyan - Viksit Bharat Ki Pechaan tih chu thupui atan hman a nih thu tarlangin a awmzia a sawifiah nghal a, Nasha Mukt Bharat Abhiyan awmzia chu Drug Free India Campaign tih niin Viksit Bharat Ki Pechaan chu Identity of Develope India tih a nih thu a tarlang a, August ni 15, 2020 ah he programme hi hawn a ni a, Champhai ah chuan nikum ah khan a vawi 1 na atan hmangin kumin hi a vawi 2 na a nih thu a sawi bawk.

Pi Thelma R. Zonunmawii, Member Secretary, NMBA Champhai chuan kalkhawmte lawmna thu a sawi a, Pu Lokeshwaran R, Superintendent of Police, Champhai chuan thu a sawi ve bawk. Anni bakah hian Pu PC Lalrawnliana, President SYMA Champhai, Pi Lalhlimthangi Khiangte, President MHIP Sub Headquarters, Champhai leh Pu K. Lalrintluanga, President MUP District Headquarter, Champhai te chuan thu an sawi ve bawk.

Drug Free Week, 2026 kharna hun pual hian Champhai Buffalo te chuan New Champhai atanga Circuit House thleng Bike Rally an nei a ni. Lawmna hun ah hian zairhuamte zai ngaihthlak a ni a, SYMA Cultural Troupe te hnam lam mawi tak an entir bawk.

He hunah kalkhawm te chuan ruihhlo leh a kaihhnawih laka fihlim turin thutiam an la a, tleirawl lai atanga ruihhlo lo khawih tawh hneh ta te testimony ngaihthlak a ni bawk.

title: CHIEF MINISTER CHAIRS STATE DISASTER MANAGEMENT AUTHORITY MEETING
content:
205/2026-2027

Aizawl, 25 June 2026:Chief Minister Shri Lalduhoma today chaired a meeting of the State Disaster Management Authority (SDMA) at the Chief Minister’s Conference Hall. The meeting was attended by Smt. Rita Massal, Member of the National Disaster Management Authority (NDMA).

The major issues discussed during the meeting included:

1. Post Disaster Needs Assessment (PDNA) Report, 2021–2025

The meeting reviewed the Post Disaster Needs Assessment (PDNA) Report covering disaster-related losses and damages in Mizoram during the period 2021–2025.

According to the report, the major disasters affecting the state included Cyclone Remal, African Swine Fever (ASF), heavy rainfall and landslides, and rodent outbreak in certain areas of Mizoram in 2025.

The report estimated the total damages incurred during the period at Rs. 656.76 crore, and the economic loss was assessed at Rs. 634.35 crore. The total requirement for recovery and reconstruction has been estimated at Rs. 879.82 crore.

2. Mizoram State Disaster Management Plan 2026

The meeting discussed and reviewed the Mizoram State Disaster Management Plan 2026 as well as the Disaster Management Plans prepared by all eleven districts of the state.

3. Strengthening of Project Management Units

The Government of Mizoram has already established Project Management Units (PMUs) for the National Landslide Risk Mitigation Project, National Earthquake Risk Mitigation Project, and the Mitigation Scheme for Forest Fire Risk Management. The meeting discussed measures to strengthen these PMUs to enable effective implementation of their respective programmes.

4. Other Disaster Management Initiatives

The meeting also deliberated on:

Continued implementation of the Emergency Response Support System (ERSS);

Selection of 178 projects to be taken up under the Recovery and Reconstruction Fund during 2024–25, involving a total expenditure of Rs. 11.44 crore; Procurement of additional Search and Rescue equipment; and declaration of African Swine Fever (ASF) as a State-Specific Disaster without a specific timeframe for the provision of Assistance from SDRF.

In addition, various disaster management-related requirements and concerns of Mizoram were presented to the NDMA Member.

The meeting was attended by Home Minister ShribK. Sapdanga, PWD Minister Shri Vanlalhlana, Health Minister Smt. Lalrinpuii, Power & Electricity Minister Shri F. Rodingliana, Food, Civil Supplies & Consumer Affairs Minister Shri B. Lalchhanzova, Disaster Management & Rehabilitation Minister Prof. Lalnilawma, along with senior government officials.

title: CHIEF MINISTER HOVIN STATE DISASTER MANAGEMENT AUTHORITY THUKHAWM
content:
1200/2026-2027

Aizawl the 25th June 2026:Vawiin khan Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma hovin State Disaster Management Authority te chu CM Conference Hall-ah an thukhawm a. Pi Rita Massal, Member, NDMA chuan a rawn telpui.

An sawiho langsarte chu:

1. Post Disaster Needs Assesment Report for 2021 - 25:

Kum 2021 - 2025 chhunga khuarel chhiatna avanga Mizoramin thil a hlohte chipchiara chhutna (PDNA) report pek a ni a. PDNA tarlan danin chhiatna thlentu lian zualte chu Cyclone Remal te, African Swine Fever te, ruah sur nasa leh leimin nasa te, kum 2025 kuma hmun thenkhata Sazu puangte an ni a.

Kha kum chhunga Mizoram hloh (total damages) zat hi Rs. 656.76 Crore niin a chhut a, Economic Loss hi Rs. 634.35 Crore hu a thlen niin a chhut a. A siam that leh vekna (overall recovery & reconstruction) atan Rs. 879.82 Crore a tul niin a chhut a ni.

2. Mizoram State Disaster Management Plan 2026 leh district 11 te Disaster Management Plan te sawifiah a ni.

3. Mizoram sorkarin Project Management Unit for National Landslide Risk Mitigation Project te, National Earthquake Risk Mitigation Project leh Mitigation Scheme for Forest Fire Risk Management te chu a lo din tawh a. He PMU hi a taka hnathawk thei tura thuam chak chungchang sawi a ni.

4. Emergency Response Support System kalpui chhunzawm zelna tur te, kum 2024 - 25 chhunga district hrang hranga recovery & reconstruction Fund atanga hnathawh tur thlan chhuah 178, a vaia Rs. 11.44 Crore senna tur te, Search & Rescue hmanraw lei belhna tur te leh ASF State Specific Disaster-a puan zui zel chungchangte pawh an sawi a.

Heng bakah hian Disaster lama Mizoram mamawh hrang hrangte Pi Rita Missal hnenah hian an thlen bawk.

Meeting-ah hian Pu K. Sapdanga, Home Minister, Pu Vanlalhlana, PWD Minister,  Pi Lalrinpuii, Health Minister,  Pu F. Rodingliana, P&E Minister, B. Lalchhanzova, Supply Minister leh  Prof. Lalnilawma, DM & R Minister te, official-te an tel a ni.

title: LAWNGTLAI DISTRICT COOPERATIVE DEVELOPMENT COMMITTEE THUKHAWM
content:
1199/2026-2027

Lawngtlai, 25th June 2026:Vawiin khan Lawngtlai District Cooperative Development Committee chuan an Chairman leh bawrhsap nibawk Pu Donny Lalruatsanga kaihhruaiin a Office Chamber-ah thutkhawm an nei.

Pu Donny Lalruatsanga chuan Cooperative Society- te hmasawnna turah kawng hrang hrangin hmalak mek a ni, a ti a. Cooperative Society - chu mi tam takin an chakna inzawmkhawm a, an nun leh khawtlangah hmasawnna an thlenna a nih angin, pawl chak tak a ni a, ram leh hnam hmasawnna thlentu pawimawh tak a ni, a ti a. Cooperative Society - kalphung mumal zawka mipuite hnenah puanzar a tul hle tiin, hemi kawngah hian 'Awareness Campaign' tam zawk neih pawh a tul hle a ti a. Bawrhsap chuan Lawngtlai District- a Cooperative Society hrang hrangte mahni zawn theuha theihtawp chhuah tur leh mimal leh ram hmasawnna thelntu ni ngei turin a chah nghal bawk.

Thutkhawmah hian LADC leh CADC-a Cooperative Department atangin report - ngaihthlak a ni a. CADC - hnuaiah 'Awareness Campaign' tum hnih kalpui tawh niin, Cooperative Society - tan cheng nuai hnih dawn a nih thu ngaihthlak a ni a. LADC - hnuaiah Registered Society - 102 awm mekin, chak taka 'Awareness Campaign' kalpui mek niin khaw 30 ah neih a nih thu ngaihthlak a ni bawk.

Thutkhawmah hian thil pawimawh tak tak sawiho a ni a. Cooperative Society - tha leh rintlak tak din zel chu thil pawimawh tak a ni tih pawmtlangin, hemi kawngah hian theihtawp chhuahho zel ni se an ti a. Awareness & Sensitisation Training' pawimawhna sawiho niin, NABARD leh MCAB - ah te ziaka thlen ni se an ti a ni. PMBJK hnuaia man tlawm zawka Damdawi zuar tura License pekchhuah tura Cooperation Department, LADC hmalakna kal mek chu bawhzui chhunzawm zel ni se an ti a, Lawngtlai District- ah Cooperative Union din tura chak zawka hmalak ni se an ti a ni. Cooperative Auditor -nei tur te, Cooperative Bank Branch - ten loan pechhuak tha turte leh 'Bana Kaih' hnuaia 'Project' kalpuiah Society mumal tak din tur ten hmalak ni se an ti bawk a ni.

Vawiin thutkhawmah hian Lawngtlai District Cooperative Development Committee Member- te an telkim tha hle a, Chairman Pu Donny Lalruatsanga chuan lawmthu sawiin hun a khar.

title: Lunglei South Bialtu MLA Pu Lalramliana Papuia’n Lungleiah Arm Wrestling-a National Medallist-te chawimawina hun a hmanpui
content:
1195/2026-2027

Lunglei, 2026 Nikir thla ni 25:Vawiin khan Dingdong Park Ramtharah Lunglei District Arm Wrestling Association (LDAWA) chuan May 29 – June 2, 2026 chhunga Gujarat-a 4th National Armwrestling & Para Armwrestling Championship khelh zawh taka Mizoram ai awha khel Lunglei player medal latu mi 6 te chawimawina hun an buatsaih a. Lunglei South Bialtu MLA Pu Lalramliana Papuia chuan Khuallian niin hun a hmanpui.

Khuallian Pu Lalramliana Papuia chuan khawvela mihring tamna ber, India ram mihring vaibelchhe 140 chuang zingah Lunglei infiam mi, arm wrestler-te’n hlawhtlinna an chang chu ropui a tih thu leh player-te chuan hma hun eng tak an neia a hriat thu sawiin, hlawhtling tura pawimawh em em chu promoter-te an nih thu leh talent nei ṭha tak takte pawh hlawhtlinna kawng zawhtir turin promoter ṭha an pawimawh thu a sawi a. Lunglei District Arm Wrestling Association-in player-te tan theih tawp an chhuahpuina rah a hmuh chu ropui a tih thu sawiin, arm wrestler-te’n hlawhtlinna an chan zel theih nan theih tawp chhuah a, a theih chin chinah ṭanpui zel a inhuam thu a sawi.

MLA Pu Lalramliana Papuia chuan hlawhtling turin mahni inthunun theihna (discipline) neih ṭhat a pawimawh thu leh tum fel tak neih lawk chu hlawhtlinna bul a nih thu sawiin, infiam mite chu tum ruhna leh inthunna nei tlat turin a chah a. Infiam mite theihna (talent) hai chhuah kawngah naupang an nih lai aṭanga hma lakpui a, ruahmanna fel tak nena enkawl a pawimawh thu sawiin, nu leh pa leh khawtlang hruaitute bakah, infiamna pawl hruaitute’n mawhphurhna thui tak an neia a hriat thu a sawi.

LDAWA President Pu K. Lalrinpuia chuan India ram pum arm wrestling intihsiakah Mizoram ai awhin Lunglei player 6 tirh an nih thu sawiin, an zavaia medal an la chu ropui leh lawmawm an tih tak zet thu a sawi a. Lunglei player-te chu Mizoramah pawh chhuanawm tak, Mizoram Arm Wrestling Association hruaitute pawhin an fak an nih thu sawiin, an kal leh zelna turah LDAWA chuan duhsakna sang ber an hlan tih a sawi.

Arm wrestler-te chawimawina inkhawm hi LDAWA Sr Vice President Lalnunpuia Tlau chuan kaihruaiin, Treasurer David J. Lalfakzuala’n hunserh a hmang a. General Secretary J. Lalzawmliana chuan report pein, National Armwrestling Championship-a Mizoram ai awha tel Lunglei player 6-in an zavaia medal an la chu thil chhinchhiah tlak, record hlu tak a nih thu a sawi a. LDAWA Executive Member Zothanpuii leh Lalnunmawii te’n player 6 te biangbiakna hun an hmang bawk.

National Armwrestling Championship 2026-a medal dawngtute chu he’ngte hi an ni:

(1)B. Lalpekhlua, Venghlun – Gold [Sub-Junior (55 Kg)]

(2)Reuben Lalhmangaihzuala, College Veng – Gold [Youth (60 Kg)]

(3)Lalbiakfela, Rahsiveng – Silver [Junior (65 Kg)] & Bronze [Senior (65 Kg)]

(4)Emily C. Rohluzuali, Venglai – Silver [Youth (50 Kg)]

(5)Biaksiamliana, Venglai – Bronze [Youth (80 Kg)]

(6)Joseph R. Lalruatdika, Electric Veng – Bronze [Senior (65 Kg)]

title: OBSERVANCE OF INTERNATIONAL DAY AGAINST DRUG ABUSE AND ILLICIT TRAFFICKING AND CLOSING OF NASHA MUKT BHARAT SAPTAH HELD IN KHAWZAWL
content:
204/2026-2027

Dated Khawzawl, the 25 June, 2026:Observance programme of International Day Against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking and closing of Nasha Mukt Bharat Saptah was held with school students today at the DC Conference Hall, Khawzawl. The programme was graced by the Deputy Commissioner of Khawzawl, Pu K. Vanlalthiana, as Chief Guest and was chaired by Pu K. Zorammuana, Addl. DC.

In his address, Deputy Commissioner Pu K. Vanlalthiana explained that the observance of the International Day Against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking on 26 June serves as a reminder of the Opium Wars. He stated that people suffering from drug addiction are among those most in need of support and assistance. He encouraged the gathering to use their knowledge and skills to help those struggling with addiction find a better path in life. He said that even if one cannot perform great deeds, a few words of encouragement and kindness can make a significant difference in the lives of those battling addiction.

He further urged the participants to send messages of hope and encouragement to those who have become trapped in addiction, lost confidence in their ability to recover and are spending their days in despair and hopelessness. Addressing the students present, he encouraged them to use their time, talents, and abilities to help safeguard the district, the state, and the community from the menace of drugs.

During the programme, Superintendent of Police Pu F. Lalbiakzauva presented a report on drug seizures in Khawzawl District. Up to 25 June 2026, authorities had seized:• 665.87 grams of heroin valued at ₹1.70 crore,• 1.972 kilograms of methamphetamine valued at ₹12.67 crore, and• 1,352.5 litres of BEDC valued at ₹10.09 lakh.

He also reported that from 1984 to 12 June 2026, a total of 2,017 persons aged between 14 and 57 years had lost their lives due to drug-related causes in Mizoram, with 20 deaths recorded up to 12 June this year alone.SDC Pi Lalthanpari presented a report on the observance of Nasha Mukt Bharat Saptah in Khawzawl and highlighted various activities undertaken by the NMBA Committee. SDO Pu Chandrakant Bagoria concluded the programme with a vote of thanks in the local Mizo language.

The International Day Against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking was proclaimed by the United Nations General Assembly on 7 December 1987 and has been observed worldwide on 26 June since 1989 to strengthen action and cooperation towards achieving a drug-free society. As 26 June falls on a government holiday this year, the programme was observed on 25 June.As part of the observance, participants took a pledge against drug abuse and joined a signature campaign. A Hlado Chham Competition on anti-drug awareness was also conducted, with Pu VL. Daniela, Sub-Hqrs YMA, and Pu Lalrinkima, Sub-Hqrs YMA, serving as judges.

title: DR. LORRAIN LALPEKLIANA CHINZAH IN DISTRICT HOSPITAL LAWNGTLAIAH  'TRAUMA CARE FACILITY' HAWNG

Dr. Lorrain Lalpekliana Chinzah chuan a mamawhtu ten tangkai leh hlawk taka an hman tura lawm taka'Trauma Care Facility'a hawn thu a sawi a. Trauma Care Facility - hawn a nih theihna turah Chief Minister leh Health Minister - ten theihtawp an chhuah tih tarlangin, an chungah lawmthu a sawi a. Lawngtlai District- in hriselna kawngah rahbi thar a kai zel chu mimal taka lawmawm a tih thu a tarlangin damlo te bakah Doctor leh thawktuten an hmangtangkai dawn hle tih sawiin, heihian damlo enkawl kawngah nasa takin hmasawnna a thlen dawn a ni, ti. District Hospital - pan tute chu mi rethei leh chanhai zawkte an nih fo thin avangin, duat leh hmangaih taka lo dawnsawng leh enkawl a pawimawh hle tiin, hemi kawnga tan la thar turin thawktute fuih a ni.

Adviser to Chief Minister chuan thawktute indaihlohna a hriatthiampui tih sawiin, hemi kawngah hian chak taka hmalak mek zel a ni, a ti a. District Hospital - mamawh tam tak zingah 'MLA Fund' atanga Nurse pakhat leh Group D- pakhat chhawr a nih tur thu a sawi a. Hriselna kawnga Lawngtlai District- hmasawnna turin tihtur tam tak a awm tih hriat a tul a, vawikhatah tih vek theih ni lo mahse a tahtawl zelin hmalak a tul tiin, MLA chuan District Hospital - a thawkte thahnem ngai taka thawk zel turin a chah nghal bawk.

Bawrhsap Pu Donny Lalruatsanga chuan'Trauma Care Facility'hawn a ni chu lawmawm a tih thu sawiin, Lawngtlai District tan hmasawnna thar a ni, a ti a. A building tihchangtlun leh zelna turah District Administration - in theihtawp a chhuah zel tur thu a tarlang a. Thawktuten hnehsawh taka vil a pawimawh dawn hle tih tarlangin, mipuite rawngbawltu an nihna hriaa, theihtawp chhuah zel turin a chah a ni.

Vawiin hun hmanna hi Dr. Paul Lalchungnunga I/C Medical Superintendent chuan kaihruaiin,thawktute an telkim tha hle a. Medical Superintendent - chuan lawmthu sawiin hun a khar.

title: Mualkawi Secondary Collection Centre te hnena minimum support price aia sanga sawhthing hralhna man tlingkhawm ₹1,99,833 hlan let
content:
1190/2026-2027

Aizawl the 25th June, 2026:Vawiin khan Agriculture & Farmers’ Welfare Minister, Pu PC Vanlalruata kaihhruaina hnuaiah, a office chamber-ah Mizoram Agriculture Marketing Board (MAMB) chuan Mualkawi Secondary Collection Centre te hnena minimum support price aia sanga sawhthing hralhna man tlingkhawm, ₹1,99,833 hlan letna hun an hmang.

Mualkawi SCC te hian Primary Society pariat an huam a. Chungte chu -- Mualkawi, Zokhawthar, Kelkang, Dilkawn, New Hruaikawn, Hruaikawn leh Buhfekzawl te an ni. Kum 2026 chhung hian Mualkawi SCC te chuan sorkar flagship programme kaltlangin an bial chhunga farmer te sawhthing thar chhuah chu auction kaltlangin an hralh chhuak a. Sawrkarin minimum support price (MSP) ₹50 per kg a ruat aia sangin sawhthing hi an hralh thei a, an hralhna rate sang ber chu ₹59 per kg lai a ni. MSP aia sanga an hralhna pawisa tlingkhawm hi ₹1,99,883/- niin, he pawisa tlingkhawm hi MAMB hnenah Mualkawi SCC chuan an chhung lut a. An pawisa chhun luh ang ang chu MAMB hian a hlan let leh a ni.

Minister Pu PC Vanlalruata, chuan thu sawiin, Mualkawi SCC ten farmer te tana rinawm taka hma an lo lak dan chu a lawmawm takzet a ni tiin, midang tana entawntlak an ni,  a ti. Sawrkarah an hlawkna te rawn chhun letna chang an hria chu ropui a tih thu sawiin,  anni anga rinawm taka farmer te tana hma la tur hi ramin a mamawh a ni, a ti a. Minister chuan MAMB anga lawmthu sawina ni pahfawmin, an SCC chuan godown thuamthat kawngah leh mamawhna hrang hrang an neih atana an hman theihnan pawisa chu hlan let an nih thu a sawi.

Pi Ramdinliani, Secretary, Agriculture & Farmers’ Welfare Department chuan thusawiin India ram hmun danga cooperative society ten farmer te thlai thar felfai taka auction an kalpui dan te leh farmer ten hlawkna an tel dan te a sawi a; chutiang anga hma la chho ve zel turin Mualkawi SCC te chu a fuih a ni.

Horticulture Department Secretary Pi Maria CT Zuali pawhin thu sawiin, sawrkarin sawhthing tharchhuah leh hralh kaihhnawih hmachhawp an neih te a sawi a ni.

Mualkawi SCC aiawhin an Chairman Pu Biaknunmawia chuan sawrkarin an SCC tana pawisa hlan letna programme an huaihawt chu lawmawm a ti a, an SCC huam chhunga farmer te sawhthing thar chhuah chu uluk takin a hmunah an enfiah thin a, dik leh felfai thei ang berin hma an la thin a ni, a ti.

He programme ah hian Pu Lalhmingmuana, Managing Director, MAMB chuan report pe a;

Pu Vanlalpeka, MAMB member leh Pu Lalfakzuala, CEO Banakaih te’n thu sawiin, Mualkawi SCC te hmalakna fakawm an tih thu an sawi.

Programme-ah hian Agriculture & Farmers’ Welfare Department leh Mizoram Agriculture Marketing Board atangin official te an tel a ni.

title: Kumin chhungin TB natna vei mi 1087 hmuhchhuah tawh a ni
content:
1189/2026-2027

Aizawl, 25 June 2026:Vawiin khan Mission Director Office chamber-ah National Health Mission (NHM) Director Dr. R. Lalchhuanawma kaihhruainain State TB Forum Meeting neih a ni.

Mission Director chuan thusawiin, Mizoram-a TB natna vei an pun zel loh nan leh TB umbo anih theihna atana ṭhahnemngai leh rim taka thawktu National Tuberculosis Elimination Programme (NTEP)-a State TB Officer leh thawktute chungah lawmthu a sawi a. TB natna umbo a nih zel theihna atana theihtawp chhuaha, taima taka thawk zel turin a chah bawk.

State TB Officer & Jt. Director (TB) Dr. Zochhuanawmi chuan Powerpoint presentation hmangin hnathawh dan hrang hrang a sawifiah a.TB damlo chhawmdawlna programme Pradhan Mantri TB Mukt Bharat Abhiyaan - Nikshay Mitra Initiative kalpui mek a nih thu a sawi a. He hmalakna atan hian Minister, MLA leh Sawrkar Official ten Food Basket hmanga TB damlo an ṭanpui mek 103 an awm thu leh TB damlo Food Basket dawng mek zat 374 an awm thu a sawi. TB natna vei inenkawl zinga 28% vel chiahin Donor an nei a, Donor neih belh zel an duh thu a tarlang bawk.

Hetiang hian January - June 2026 chhunga TB report tlangpui tarlan a ni:

1. TB rinhlelh test zat - 612462. TB damlo hmuhchhuah zat - 10873. Kum 14 hnuai lam TB vei zat - 68 (6.5%)4. Kum 14 chunglam TB vei zat 977 (93.5%)5. Mipa TB vei zat - 600 (57%)6. Hmeichhia TB vei zat - 445 (43%)7. MDR - TB hmuhchhuah zat - 478. Chuap TB vei zat - 520 (49%)9. Chuap pawn lam TB vei zat - 538 (51%)10. TB-HIV vei kawp hmuhchhuah zat - 9211. TB vanga thi - 7112. TB tihdam zat - 86.49%

TB umbona atana campaign kalpui mek TB Mukt Bharat 100 Days campaign-ah khua leh vengah screening runpui neih thin a ni a, heta inentir tam ber hi kum 50 chunglam an ni. TB veiah hian kum 15 leh kum 45 inkar an tam ber avangin screening neihah kum valai zawk te an kal ṭha lo ṭhin chu harsatna lian tak a ni tih sawi lan a ni a. College zirlai te, office a thawk kum valai te, kum 15 leh 45 inkar hrim hrim te chu a tam thei ang ber screen an nih theih nan hmalak a ngaih thu tarlan a ni.

TB damlo hospital admit mamawhte tan Sawrkar hospital-ah ICU khum 1 leh general ward-ah khum 5 te hauhsak an nih theihna turin hmalak ni se an ti a.

TB damlo chaw ṭha hmanga chhawmdawltu Donor zawn kawngah hmalakna tur hrang hrang te sawiho a ni bawk.

Meeting-ah hian Director of Health Services Dr. Lily Chhakchhuak, DHME Addl. Director Dr. Catherine Ngurbiakveli, School Education Department Jt. Director Pu PC. Lalngaizuala te bakah MSACS, ICT Dept., ZMC aiawh leh NHM Official engemaw zat telin, TB Survivor sawm bik ṭhahnem tak an tel bawk a ni.

title: Pu B.Lalchhanzova’n  LR&S Department One Day Review Meeting a hmanpui
content:
1188/2026-2027

Aizawl the 25th June, 2026:Vawin khan Land Revenue & Settlement Department chuan Folkland Park Conference Room ah State level review meeting neiin an department hmalak mekna te leh hmathlir bakah an hnathawh kawnga harsatna hrang hrangte sutkian dan tur an sawi ho.

LR&S Minister Pu B. Lalchhanzova chuan thu sawiin, kum hnih leh a chanve chauh ama ho a an thawh ho chhungin thawktute tanrualna avangin mipui pawhin thil tha tih tum leh hmasawn tum hmuha LR&S department an hmu nia a hriat thu sawiin lawmawm a tih thu a sawi. Revenue Minister chuan LR&S Dept laka mipuiin rinna an nghaha, rinngam an nihna lantir turin amah Revenue Minister berin hma a hruai a, ama pualin ram khawi hmunah mah a in pe lo tih sawiin, a thawhpuite pawh chulamah chuan a hnung zui ngam turin a fuih.

Department hmalakna tih chak zel dan tur te, siamthatna hna kalpui mek ennawnna te leh kalphung siam that la tul awm te bakah mipui rawngbawlna kawnga district hrang hrang te harsatna tawh te ngaihthlak leh sawi hona hun siam a ni chu lawmawm a tih thu sawiin, Minister chuan State Level review meeting hi ama huna an neih vawi hnihna niin, hei hi kum tin neih thin tur a nih thu a sawi.

Minister chuan Revenue department hi Mizoram state in ama puala sum a hmuh theihna State Own Revenue source pawimawh tak a ni a, mipui te pawh in Mizoram state in ama puala sum a thawhchhuah pawimawhna an hrethiam chho zel a,   kum kal ta a Stamps & Registration bakah  Land Tax leh Non Tax  atanga Department sum  lakluh pawh Vaibelchhe 40.23 a tling chho ta mek a, a pung chho tha hle a, heng sum te hi Mizoram Sawrkar ta liau liau, state mamawha hman tur a ni, a ti. Minister chuan Ram hlutna chhutna (Land Valuation) mumal nei turin MZU Economics Dept te thiamna hman tangkai mek a ni bawk a, tax pe lo te chhuichhuah leh umzui hna pawh online tax payment 2.0 hmangin chak taka enzui mek a ni, a ti bawk.

Pu B. Lalchhanzova chuan mipui ten LR&S dept min mamawhna a sang chho zel a, min beisei ang diak diak la thleng phaklo mah ila, rim takin hna an thawk mek zel a ni tih hriat sak turin a ngen a ni.

Pu B.Lalchhanzova chuan LR&S department chu thawktu za zela sawmnga chauh hnawhkhah ni chunga hna thawk mek an nih thu sawiin, hnathawh tur a tam bakah, mipuiin an mamawhna a sang a; chung karah chuan technology nen tangkawp zelin, thawktute pawhin an indaihlohna  hmulian loin, mahni tawkah theihtawp chhuah theuh turin a chah bawk a ni. Revenue kaihhnawihah office leh in lamah amah hmu an tam thin thu Minister chuan a sawi a. Amah  hmuh tum tu an tlem hunah LR&S Department-in revenue reforms an kalpui mek chu a hlawhtling a tih ngam ang, Minister chuan  a ti.

Review Meeting hi LR&S Secretary Pu Vanlalmawia, IAS chuan a kaihruai a, Administrative Department bakah Directorate leh District tin Assistant Commissioner-te hnen atangin presentation ngaihthlak a ni. He one day review meeting-ah hian LR&S Department a Secretariat, Directorate leh District a thuneitute an tel kim hle a ni.

title: SERCHHIP-AH NASHA MUKT BHARAT SAPTAH KHARNA HUN HMAN A NI
content:
1186/2026-2027

Dated Serchhip, the 25thJune 2026:Vawiin khan Nasha Mukt Bharat Saptah (Drug-Free Week) Serchhip Distirct a June 22 atanga hman chu a kharna hun Serchhip DC Pisapui Hall-ah hman a ni a. Serchhip District Additional Deputy Commissioner Pi Dorothy Muansangi’n hun hmanpuiin, SDO (Sadar) Pi Rebecca Laldinmawii Hrahsel-in kharna inkhawm hi a kaihruai a, Serchhip Superintendent of Police Pu Gavit Gogna bakah sawrkar department hrang hrang aiawhte, MJA leh khawtlang hruaitute he hunah hian an tel a ni.

Addl. DC Pi Dorothy Muansangi chuan thu sawiin Serchhip District-a NMBA hnuaia ruihhlo dona atana hmalakna lo neih tawh dan leh neih mek dante a sawi a. Ruihhlo do turin mipuite kan tanrual a ngaih thu leh naupang te atanga bul tan a pawimawh thu sawiin, nu leh pa leh zirtirtu ten naupangte hnenah ruihhlo that lohziate inzirtirna uar lehzuala neih a tul thu a sawi a. Mizoram chu khawvela ruihhloa sumdawnna hmunpui (Golden Triangle) bula awm a nih avangin ruihhlo do tura mipuite kan tanrual zel a pawimawh thu a sawi bawk.

Serchhip SP Gavit Gogna pawhin Serchhip Police Department te chu ruihhlo do turin engtik lai pawha an inrin reng thu sawiin nikum June 26 leh tun inkarah Serchhip district-ah ruihhlo NDPS Act hnuaiah Case 6 ziahluh tawh niin 40% te chu man an nih tawh thu a sawi a, Heroin 1.3 Kg leh Ganja 2.9 Kg zet man niin zu pawh 800 litre zet nimin maiah man a nih thu a sawi a; chak taka hmalak zel a nih theih nan Awareness programme leh thawktute intuaitharna Training pawh vawi eng emaw zat neih a nih tawh thu a sawi bawk.

Vawiin hun hmannaah hian Election Officer Pi Lalchhanhimi   kaihhruainain Ruihhlo laka fihlim tur leh ruihhlo do na kawnga theihtawp chhuah tura intiamna/thu chham rualna neih niin Serchhip District Information & Public Relations Officer (DIPRO) Pu R. Vanneihtluanga hnen atangin Serchhip district chhunga Drug-Free Week hman dan hrang hrangte report ngaihthlak a ni.

June 17 - 26 thleng awh tur he Nasha Mukt Bharat Saptah hi Serchhip-ah chuan June 22 - 25 chhung hian ngawrh taka hman niin, mipui punkhawmna hmunahte Awareness Campaign, Pledge leh Signature Campaign te neih a ni a, serchhip district chhunga zirna In hrang hrang 120 chuanga zirlaite hnenah ‘Ruihhlo Zunzam- Drug Awareness Film’ chhuah a ni bawk a. Counselling Session, Slogan Writing Competition, Walkathon, Yoga Meditation, Human Chains Against Drugs leh Two Wheeler Rally te he Week chhung hian neih niin mipui leh zirlai thahnem tak an tel a ni.

title: KHAWZAWLAH NASHA MUKT BHARAT SAPTAH KHARNA LEH INTERNATIONAL DAY AGAINST DRUG ABUSE AND ILLICIT TRAFFICKING PUALIN HUN HMAN A NI
content:
1183/2026-2027

Dated Khawzawl, the 25thJune, 2026 :Vawiin khan Nasha Mukt Bharat Saptah kharna leh ‘International Day Against Drug Abuse And Illicit Trafficking’ pualin Khawzawl DC Conference Hall-ah zirlai naupangte nen hun hman a ni a. He hun hi Khawzawl Bawrhsap Pu K. Vanlalthiana’n khuallian niin a hmanpui a, Addl DC Pu K. Zorammuana’n a kaihruai.

Bawrhsap Pu K. Vanlalthiana chuan International Day Against Drug Abuse And Illicit Trafficking June 26-a kan hmanna chhan chu Opium War hriatrengna atan a nih thu a sawi a. Ruihhlo avanga harsatna tawkte chu khawvela tanpui ngaite zinga tanpui ngai ber pawl an nih thu sawiin, kalkhawmte chu ruihhlo ngaiten nunkawng tha zawk an zawh theih nan an finna leh thiamna te hmang turin a fuih a. Thil ropui tak kan tihsak theih loh pawhin ruihhlo ngaite enhranga diriam mai lovin tawngkam tha leh fuihna thu tawite pawhin a chhanchhuak thei a ni, a ti a. Thalai tamtak ruihhlo bawiha tang, chhanchhuah theiha inngai tawh lo, mi zinga nungtlak pawha inngai tawh lo, khawhar leh beidawng taka hun hmang mekte tan anmahni fuihna thu tha tlawmte tal vawiinah hian thawn theuh turin kalkhawmte a sawm a. Vawiina zirlai duhawm tak takte hian kan ram, kan hnam leh kan district ruihhlo laka kan filhim theih nan an hun, an thiamna leh finna te hmang turin a fuih.

Vawiin hunah hian SP Pu F. Lalbiakzauva’n Khawzawla ruihhlo man dan report a pe a. Kumin June 25 thleng khan Heroin 665.87 grams, Rs. 1,70,00,000 man hu, Meth 1.972 kgs, Rs. 12,67,36,000 man hu leh BEDC 1352.5 litres, Rs. 10,09,125 man hu man a ni tawh a. Mizoramah kum 1984 atanga June 12, 2026 thleng khan ruihhlo vangin kum 14-57 inkar mi 2017-in nunna an chan tawh a, kumin June 12 thleng khan mi 20 in nunna an chan tawh tih a tarlang bawk a. SDC Pi Lalthanpari’n Khawzawlah Nasha Mukt Bharat SaptahHman dan leh NMBA Committee hmalakna hrang hrang report a pe a. SDO Pu Chandrakant Bagoria'an Mizo tawngin lawmthu sawiin hun a khar.

‘International Day Against Drug Abuse And Illicit Trafficking’ hi UN General Assembly December 7, 1987 a rel a nih angin kum 1989 atang khan June 26-ah khawvel puma drugs hmansual leh drugs hmanga sumdawnna dona ni atan hman tan a ni a. June 26 hi sawrkar chawlh a nih avangin vawiin June 25 hian hun hman a ni.

Vawiin ruihhlo hmansual leh a tawlhruk dona niah hian ruihhlo ti lo tura intiamna pledge lak niin, signature campaign neih a ni bawk a. Ruihhlo do lam hawi Hlado Chham intihsiakna neih a ni a, judge-ah Pu VL. Daniela, Sub-Hqrs YMA leh Pu Lalrinkima, Sub-Hqrs YMA te ruat an ni.

Nasha Mukt Bharat Saptah chhunga intihsiakna buatsaiha lawmman dawngtute chu a hnuaiah tarlan an ni.

title: “Even If Drugs Are Within Reach, Have the Courage to Say No. Report Drug Peddlers and Traffikers, Let Them Face the Punishment They Deserve.” – Chief Minister Lalduhoma
content:
203/2026-2027

Aizawl, 25 June 2026:The International Day Against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking was observed today at Dawrpui Multipurpose Hall, with Chief Minister Shri Lalduhoma attending as the Chief Guest.

Addressing the gathering, particularly the students present, the Chief Minister urged everyone to have the courage to reject drugs even when they are easily accessible.

“Even if drugs are within your reach, do not experiment with them. They are tools of temptation that can destroy your life. Many people begin by trying drugs, believing they will not become addicted, but soon find themselves enslaved by addiction. Drugs destroy families and often lead people to an untimely grave,” he said.

The programme was attended by Smt. Lalrinpuii, Minister for Social Welfare, Women & Child Development; Shri Lalnghinglova Hmar, Minister for Excise & Narcotics; Shri K. Laldawngliana, MLA; Shri Khilli Ram Meena, Chief Secretary; senior government officials; and a large number of students. Participants reaffirmed their commitment to remain free from drugs and to continue the fight against substance abuse.

Highlights of the Chief Minister’s Address:

1. Significant Progress in Combating Drug Abuse

The Chief Minister recalled that when the International Day Against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking was observed in 2024, scenes of drug users wandering on the streets of Aizawl and district headquarters were common and often circulated widely on social media.

Since then, the Government of Mizoram, civil society organisations, churches, and community leaders have worked together to address both the supply and demand sides of the drug problem. Measures undertaken include the setting up of the Security Coordination Committee, revival of the Core Committee on Drug Prevention, implementation of targeted operations, awareness campaigns among students, media outreach initiatives, and rehabilitation programmes such as the Hulhhliap Short Stay initiative.

The Young Mizo Association (YMA) declared a year-long anti-drug campaign and actively conducted Anti-Drugs Squad operations, while churches were encouraged to intensify awareness efforts within their congregations.

The Government also partnered with rehabilitation centres, including the Sethawn Jordan Centre in collaboration with the Mizoram Synod.

As a result of these collective efforts, the Chief Minister noted that visible drug abuse on the streets has declined significantly and many individuals have successfully undergone rehabilitation and returned to normal life.

2. Prevention Is the Best Protection:

The Chief Minister highlighted Mizoram’s geographical proximity to regions known for illicit drug production and trafficking, making the State particularly vulnerable.

“While law enforcement efforts must continue, it is impossible to eliminate the threat entirely. Therefore, we must develop the strength to refuse drugs even when they are readily available. Experts agree that the best protection against drug addiction is never trying drugs in the first place,” he said.

He reiterated his appeal to students and young people to avoid experimenting with drugs under any circumstances.

3. Report Drug Peddlers and traffickers:

The Chief Minister strongly condemned those who profit from the sale of drugs, describing them as individuals whose actions destroy lives, families, and communities.

Referring to recent cases of drug trafficking incidences, he warned that traffickers often exploit trust, friendship, and the Mizo tradition of helping others by persuading people to carry packages containing narcotics. Many innocent individuals have consequently been arrested and imprisoned.

He urged citizens to report any suspected drug peddlers or trafficking activities to the authorities.

“If we know or suspect anyone involved in drug trafficking, we must report them. Let them face the punishment they deserve,” he said.

4. Responding to Emerging Challenges:

The theme for this year’s observance is “Persisting Issues, New Challenges, Innovative Responses.”

The Chief Minister noted that the emergence of synthetic drugs, increasingly sophisticated criminal networks, and the use of advanced technologies for trafficking present new challenges in the fight against drugs. He warned that technologies such as drones could potentially be used for drug trafficking in the future.

He stressed the need for continuous learning, innovation, and evidence-based awareness campaigns to stay ahead of evolving threats. “Protecting Mizoram from the scourge of drugs and guiding our youth away from addiction is not only a social responsibility but also an essential task in safeguarding our State and our people,” he said.

Special Recognition Awards:

During the programme, awards were presented to individuals and organisations for their outstanding contributions in various fields:

Category 1: Sports* Cindy Remruatpuii Colney (Footballer)* Samuel Zadeng (Boxer)

Category 2: CmRehabilitation* New Life Home Society, Jeriko Hostel, for providing care and rehabilitation services to vulnerable girls, with more than 5,000 beneficiaries assisted.

Category 3: Drug Rehabilitation and Recovery Services* Jimmy LNS Tluanga, for 23 years of service in rehabilitation and recovery support.

* B. Lalrinpuii, for seven years of dedicated service to recovering drug users.

The observance was jointly organised by the Departments of Social Welfare, Women & Child Development, Health & Family Welfare, Excise & Narcotics, Information & Public Relations, Higher & Technical Education, School Education, and other partner organisations including Assam Rifles, BSF, MSACS, CYMA, MKHC, MHIP, MDUF, and FINGODAP.
